enriched_points.expanded_content: Zinc contamination in soils poses significant environmental challenges, hindering agricultural productivity and ecosystem health, necessitating effective remediation strategies.
Willow and herbaceous species exhibit remarkable extraction efficiency, effectively accumulating zinc from contaminated soils, thereby reducing toxic levels and restoring soil quality.
Utilizing phytoremediation with these species can revitalize degraded agricultural lands, making them suitable for cultivation again, thus enhancing food security.
Ongoing studies demonstrate sustained improvements in soil health and reduced zinc levels, highlighting the effectiveness and reliability of phytoremediation practices over time.
